title: Partial-Credit RL for Reliable Code Generation with Small Language Models (WIP) — LCTES '26 artifact
description: Source code, training pipeline, and evaluation scripts for the LCTES '26 WIP paper "Partial-Credit RL for Reliable Code Generation with Small Language Models (WIP)." The work introduces a partial-credit functional reward that distinguishes near-miss code-generation outcomes (syntax error, crash, missing output, partial test success) and assigns intermediate credit, paired with a Bandit static-analysis security term. On DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B evaluated on 100 stdin-style APPS+ prompts, partial-credit PPO improves syntax validity to 63% and ≥1-test-pass to 9%, while binary-reward PPO regresses below the SFT baseline. A binary-to-partial-credit curriculum further outperforms training with partial credit from scratch.
